theme: a creature that regrows what you cut off
title: Wound-Ring: A Regenerating Cellular Organism
innovation_hook: Regeneration is impossible without an internally reconstructable coordinate system, so the rule must maintain positional information, not just a shape.
strategies: do-nothing baseline: the identity rule; the seed cell never spreads (reproduces the evaluator's do-nothing reference, ~0.1)

copy-and-freeze (the trap): simulate one forward growth trace and memorize per-pattern transitions with a fixed neighbor-priority order, then never revisit an already-alive cell; usually grows the shape once but a reopened wound's multi-neighbor context was never seen, so it heals wrong or not at all

distance-relaxation invariant (the insight): each cell's correct state is 1 + the minimum ring value among its live, non-wall neighbors (the seed self-sustains); this invariant holds everywhere in the target, is order-independent under asynchronous sweeps, and re-derives any wound's interior from surviving local context alone -- true self-repair

invariant + broad drilled coverage: additionally harvest the relaxation rule's outputs over many synthetic hole-and-heal drills (varied sizes/positions) so the emitted lookup table generalizes to wounds it never specifically saw, closing residual gaps left by a single growth trace
